Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows significantly elevated creatinine, suggesting impaired renal function. Mildly elevated ALT may indicate some liver involvement. Lipid profile reveals high LDL, triglycerides, and HDL, indicating dyslipidemia. Globulin is markedly low, which may warrant further evaluation for immunodeficiency or protein loss. Other parameters are within normal limits. Recommend clinical correlation and further nephrological and hepatic assessment.
